mechanism_narrative: A 3-month acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strengthening aggregate labor income, driving consumption expectations and S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS 3-month change is not positively correlated with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, further increases in the rate raise real discount rates and tighten financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising unemployment and elevated federal funds rates generate a credit-constrained recessionary dynamic that amplifies downside equity risk beyond either factor alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are non-negative in > 55% of triggered episodes during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, month-over-month declines in housing starts signal that monetary transmission is actively suppressing residential investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not coincide with negative 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of high-rate episodes over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals rising corporate operating leverage and earnings momentum, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO 3-month changes show negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nominal retail sales growth reflects current consumption strength and nominal revenue expansion; three-month increases in retail sales predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS 3-month momentum fails to produce positive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds a threshold of 5% annualized, equity risk premia expand due to fears of persistent monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days following the release.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in > 50% of triggered CPI releases over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between accelerating GDP growth and falling unemployment creates a virtuous cycle of income and spending that boosts equity valuations beyond linear effects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2% annualized and UNRATE < 4%
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered quarters over any 3-year window.
